description: Native octoclaw / DTBClaw tools for edge devices via MCP WS Hub. Regenerate with: cargo run --bin export_dtbclaw_edge_catalog

tools.description: Safely check/apply a unified diff to the current git repository, optionally staging and committing.

commit_message.description: If provided (and dry_run=false), stage all changes and create a git commit with this message.

dry_run.description: If true, only checks whether the patch would apply cleanly (no changes made).

patch.description: Unified diff text (e.g. output of `git diff`).

tools.name: Edge: apply_patch

tools.description: Execute a tool in the background and return a job ID immediately. Use this for long-running operations where you don't want to block. Check results with bg_status or wait for auto-injection in the next turn. Background tools have a 600-second maximum timeout.

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the tool

tool.description: Name of the tool to execute in the background

tools.name: Edge: bg_run

tools.description: Query the status of a background job by ID, or list all jobs if no ID provided. Returns job status (running/complete/failed), result output, and elapsed time.

job_id.description: Optional job ID to query. If omitted, returns all jobs.

tools.name: Edge: bg_status

tools.description: Web/browser automation with pluggable backends (agent-browser, rust-native, computer_use). Supports DOM actions plus optional OS-level actions (mouse_move, mouse_click, mouse_drag, key_type, key_press, screen_capture) through a computer-use sidecar. Use 'snapshot' to map interactive elements to refs (@e1, @e2). Enforces browser.allowed_domains for open actions.

action.description: Browser action to perform (OS-level actions require backend=computer_use)

compact.description: For snapshot: remove empty structural elements

fill_value.description: For find with fill action: value to fill

find_action.description: For find: action to perform on found element

interactive_only.description: For snapshot: only show interactive elements

key.description: Key to press (Enter, Tab, Escape, etc.)

text.description: Text to type or wait for

tools.name: Edge: browser

tools.description: Open an approved HTTPS URL in a browser. Security constraints: allowlist-only domains, no local/private hosts, no scraping.
tools.name: Edge: browser_open

tools.description: Inspect and update configurable ACK emoji reaction policies for Telegram/Discord/Lark/Feishu under [channels_config.ack_reaction]. Supports enabling/disabling reactions, setting emoji pools, and rule-based conditions.
tools.name: Edge: channel_ack_config

tools.description: Check current rate limit and quota status for AI providers. Returns available providers, rate-limited providers, quota remaining, and estimated reset time. Use this when user asks about model availability or when you encounter rate limit errors.

provider.description: Specific provider to check (optional). Examples: openai, gemini, anthropic. If omitted, checks all providers.

tools.name: Edge: check_provider_quota

tools.description: Search file contents by regex pattern within the workspace. Supports ripgrep (rg) with grep fallback. Output modes: 'content' (matching lines with context), 'files_with_matches' (file paths only), 'count' (match counts per file). Example: pattern='fn main', include='*.rs', output_mode='content'.

context_after.description: Lines of context after each match (content mode only)

context_before.description: Lines of context before each match (content mode only)

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 1000

multiline.description: Enable multiline matching (ripgrep only, errors on grep fallback)

path.description: Directory to search in, relative to workspace root. Defaults to '.'

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to search for

tools.name: Edge: content_search

tools.description: Create a scheduled cron job (shell or agent) with cron/at/every schedules. Use job_type='agent' with a prompt to run the AI agent on schedule. Use schedule.kind='at' for one-time reminders/delayed sends (recommended). Agent jobs with schedule.kind='cron' or schedule.kind='every' are recurring and require explicit recurring confirmation. To deliver output to a channel (Discord, Telegram, Slack, Mattermost, QQ, Napcat, Lark, Feishu, Email), set delivery={"mode":"announce","channel":"discord","to":"<channel_id_or_chat_id>"}. This is the preferred tool for sending scheduled/delayed messages to users via channels.

approved.description: Set true to explicitly approve medium/high-risk shell commands in supervised mode

delivery.description: Delivery config to send job output to a channel. Example: {"mode":"announce","channel":"discord","to":"<channel_id>"}

best_effort.description: If true, delivery failure does not fail the job

mode.description: Set to 'announce' to deliver output to a channel

to.description: Target: Discord channel ID, Telegram chat ID, Slack channel, etc.

recurring_confirmed.description: Required for agent recurring schedules (schedule.kind='cron' or 'every'). Set true only when recurring behavior is intentional.

schedule.description: Schedule object: {kind:'cron',expr,tz?} recurring | {kind:'at',at} one-time | {kind:'every',every_ms} recurring interval

tools.name: Edge: cron_add

tools.description: List all scheduled cron jobs
tools.name: Edge: cron_list

tools.description: Remove a cron job by id
tools.name: Edge: cron_remove

tools.description: Force-run a cron job immediately and record run history

approved.description: Set true to explicitly approve medium/high-risk shell commands in supervised mode

tools.name: Edge: cron_run

tools.description: List recent run history for a cron job
tools.name: Edge: cron_runs

tools.description: Patch an existing cron job (schedule, command, prompt, enabled, delivery, model, etc.)

approved.description: Set true to explicitly approve medium/high-risk shell commands in supervised mode

tools.name: Edge: cron_update

tools.description: Control Cursor via its CLI. Key operation: 'agent' runs Cursor Agent headlessly in terminal (no IDE needed) — it reads, writes, and runs shell commands on the workspace autonomously. Other operations: open, goto, diff, prompt, install_extension, uninstall_extension, list_extensions, new_window, status, help. Requires the `cursor` command on PATH. Call with {"operation":"help"} for full usage.

force.description: Auto-approve all agent actions without prompting (for 'agent', default: true)

headless.description: Run agent in headless mode (--print, no terminal window). Default: false (opens a visible terminal)

message.description: Prompt or instruction text to send to Cursor AI (for 'prompt')

mode.description: How to use the prompt in Cursor (for 'prompt'): 'composer' = Cmd+I multi-file edits, 'chat' = Cmd+L ask questions, 'edit' = Cmd+K inline code edits, 'rules' = save as project rule. Default: 'composer'

operation.description: Cursor CLI operation. 'agent' starts Cursor Agent in a terminal. 'agent_status' polls agent progress and reads output. Use 'help' for full guide.

output_format.description: Output format for agent responses (for 'agent', default: 'text')

reuse_window.description: Reuse existing window instead of opening a new one (for 'open', default: true)

tail.description: Number of output lines to return (for 'agent_status', default: 50)

wait.description: Wait for the file to be closed before returning (for 'open', default: false)

workspace.description: Workspace directory for agent to operate on (for 'agent'/'agent_status', defaults to current workspace)

tools.name: Edge: cursor_cli

tools.description: Extract plain text from a DOCX (Word) file in the workspace. Returns all readable text content. No formatting, images, or charts.

path.description: Path to the DOCX file. Relative paths resolve from workspace.

tools.name: Edge: docx_read

tools.description: Estimate quota cost (tokens, requests) for an operation before executing it. Useful for warning user if operation may exhaust quota or when planning parallel tool calls.

parallel_count.description: Number of parallel operations (if applicable, default: 1)

tools.name: Edge: estimate_quota_cost

tools.description: Edit a file by replacing text in a file. Exact matching is preferred; if exact matching fails, whitespace-flexible line matching is used. Sensitive files (for example .env and key material) are blocked by default.

new_string.description: The replacement text (empty string to delete the matched text)

old_string.description: The text to find and replace. Exact matching is attempted first; if no exact match is found, whitespace-flexible line matching is attempted.

path.description: Path to the file. Relative paths resolve from workspace; outside paths require policy allowlist.

tools.name: Edge: file_edit

tools.description: Read file contents with line numbers. Supports partial reading via offset and limit. Extracts text from PDF; other binary files are read with lossy UTF-8 conversion. Sensitive files (for example .env and key material) are blocked by default.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return (default: all)

path.description: Path to the file. Relative paths resolve from workspace; outside paths require policy allowlist.

tools.name: Edge: file_read

tools.description: Write contents to a file in the workspace. Sensitive files (for example .env and key material) are blocked by default.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file. Relative paths resolve from workspace; outside paths require policy allowlist.

tools.name: Edge: file_write

tools.description: Perform structured Git operations (status, diff, log, branch, commit, add, checkout, stash). Provides parsed JSON output and integrates with security policy for autonomy controls.

files.description: File or path to diff (for 'diff' operation, default: '.')

limit.description: Number of log entries (for 'log' operation, default: 10)

tools.name: Edge: git_operations

tools.description: Search for files matching a glob pattern within the workspace. Returns a sorted list of matching file paths relative to the workspace root. Examples: '**/*.rs' (all Rust files), 'src/**/mod.rs' (all mod.rs in src).

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '**/*.rs', 'src/**/mod.rs'

tools.name: Edge: glob_search

tools.description: Make HTTP requests to external APIs. Supports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS methods. Security constraints: allowlist-only domains, no local/private hosts, configurable timeout/response size limits, and optional env-backed credential profiles.

body.description: Optional request body (for POST, PUT, PATCH requests)

credential_profile.description: Optional profile name from [http_request.credential_profiles]. Lets the harness inject credentials from environment variables without passing raw tokens in tool arguments.

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers as key-value pairs (e.g., {"Authorization": "Bearer token", "Content-Type": "application/json"})

method.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS)

url.description: HTTP or HTTPS URL to request

tools.name: Edge: http_request

tools.description: Read image file metadata (format, dimensions, size) and optionally return base64-encoded data.

include_base64.description: Include base64-encoded image data in output (default: false)

path.description: Path to the image file (absolute or relative to workspace)

tools.name: Edge: image_info

tools.description: Manage auth profiles: list all profiles with token status, switch active profile for a provider, or refresh expired OAuth tokens. Use when user asks about accounts, tokens, or when you encounter expired/rate-limited credentials.

action.description: Action to perform: 'list' shows all profiles, 'switch' changes active profile, 'refresh' renews OAuth tokens

profile.description: Profile name to switch to (for 'switch' action). E.g., 'default', 'work', 'personal'.

provider.description: Provider name (e.g., 'gemini', 'openai-codex', 'anthropic'). Required for switch and refresh.

tools.name: Edge: manage_auth_profile

tools.description: Remove a memory by key. Use to delete outdated facts or sensitive data. Returns whether the memory was found and removed.

key.description: The key of the memory to forget

tools.name: Edge: memory_forget

tools.description: Store an observation entry in observation memory for long-horizon context continuity.

key.description: Optional custom key. Auto-generated when omitted.

observation.description: Observation to capture (fact, pattern, or running context signal)

tools.name: Edge: memory_observe

tools.description: Search long-term memory for relevant facts, preferences, or context. Returns scored results ranked by relevance.

query.description: Keywords or phrase to search for in memory

tools.name: Edge: memory_recall

tools.description: Store a fact, preference, or note in long-term memory. Use category 'core' for permanent facts, 'daily' for session notes, 'conversation' for chat context, or a custom category name.

category.description: Memory category: 'core' (permanent), 'daily' (session), 'conversation' (chat), or a custom category name. Defaults to 'core'.

tools.name: Edge: memory_store

tools.description: Manage default model settings, scenario routes, classification rules, delegate profiles, and agent team/subagent orchestration controls. Designed for natural-language runtime reconfiguration (enable/disable, strategy, and capacity tuning).

agentic.description: Enable tool-call loop mode for delegate agent

allowed_tools.description: Allowed tools for agentic delegate mode (string or string array)

api_key.description: Optional API key override for scenario route or delegate agent

capabilities.description: Capability tags for automatic agent selection (string or string array)

classification_enabled.description: When true, upsert classification rule for this hint; false removes it

enabled.description: Enable or disable a delegate profile for selection/invocation

hint.description: Scenario hint name (for example: conversation, coding, reasoning)

keywords.description: Classification keywords for upsert_scenario (string or string array)

max_concurrent_subagents.description: Maximum number of concurrently running background sub-agents (positive integer, no hard-coded upper cap)

max_iterations.description: Maximum tool-call iterations for agentic delegate mode

max_team_agents.description: Maximum number of delegate profiles activated for teams (positive integer, no hard-coded upper cap)

patterns.description: Classification literal patterns for upsert_scenario (string or string array)

priority.description: Priority value. For scenarios: classifier order (higher runs first). For upsert_agent: delegate selection priority.

remove_classification.description: When remove_scenario, whether to remove matching classification rule (default true)

subagents_auto_activate.description: Enable/disable automatic sub-agent selection when agent is omitted or 'auto'

subagents_inflight_penalty.description: Sub-agent score penalty per in-flight task

subagents_load_window_secs.description: Recent-event window for sub-agent load balancing (seconds)

subagents_queue_poll_ms.description: Poll interval while waiting for sub-agent capacity (milliseconds)

subagents_queue_wait_ms.description: How long to wait for sub-agent capacity before failing (milliseconds)

subagents_recent_failure_penalty.description: Sub-agent score penalty per recent failure in the load window

subagents_recent_selection_penalty.description: Sub-agent score penalty per recent assignment in the load window

system_prompt.description: Optional system prompt override for delegate agent

teams_auto_activate.description: Enable/disable automatic team-agent selection when agent is omitted or 'auto'

teams_inflight_penalty.description: Team score penalty per in-flight task

teams_load_window_secs.description: Recent-event window for team load balancing (seconds)

teams_recent_failure_penalty.description: Team score penalty per recent failure in the load window

teams_recent_selection_penalty.description: Team score penalty per recent assignment in the load window

transport.description: Optional route transport override for upsert_scenario (auto, websocket, sse)

tools.name: Edge: model_routing_config

tools.description: Preview or execute merge-first migration from OpenClaw (memory + config + agents) without overwriting existing OctoClaw data.

action.description: preview runs a dry-run report; migrate applies merge changes

include_config.description: Whether to migrate provider/channels/agents config (default true)

include_memory.description: Whether to migrate memory entries (default true)

tools.name: Edge: openclaw_migration

tools.description: Extract plain text from a PDF file in the workspace. Returns all readable text. Image-only or encrypted PDFs return an empty result. Requires the 'rag-pdf' build feature.

path.description: Path to the PDF file. Relative paths resolve from workspace; outside paths require policy allowlist.

tools.name: Edge: pdf_read

tools.description: Extract plain text from a PPTX (PowerPoint) file in the workspace. Returns all readable text content from all slides. No formatting, images, or charts.

path.description: Path to the PPTX file. Relative paths resolve from workspace.

tools.name: Edge: pptx_read

tools.description: Manage background processes: spawn long-running commands, check output, and terminate them

action.description: Action to perform: spawn a process, list all, get output, or kill

command.description: Shell command to run in background (required for 'spawn')

id.description: Process ID returned by spawn (required for 'output' and 'kill')

tools.name: Edge: process

tools.description: Manage OctoClaw proxy settings (scope: environment | octoclaw | services), including runtime and process env application

all_proxy.description: Fallback proxy URL for all protocols

clear_env.description: When action=disable, clear process proxy environment variables

no_proxy.description: Comma-separated string or array of NO_PROXY entries

services.description: Comma-separated string or array of service selectors used when scope=services

tools.name: Edge: proxy_config

tools.description: Send a Pushover notification to your device. Uses PUSHOVER_TOKEN/PUSHOVER_USER_KEY from process environment first, then falls back to .env.
tools.name: Edge: pushover

tools.description: Manage scheduled shell-only tasks. Actions: create/add/once/list/get/cancel/remove/pause/resume. WARNING: This tool creates shell jobs whose output is only logged, NOT delivered to any channel. To send a scheduled message to Discord/Telegram/Slack/Mattermost/QQ/Lark/Feishu/Email, use the cron_add tool with job_type='agent' and a delivery config like {"mode":"announce","channel":"discord","to":"<channel_id>"}.

approved.description: Set true to explicitly approve medium/high-risk shell commands in supervised mode

command.description: Shell command to execute. Required for create/add/once.

run_at.description: Absolute RFC3339 time for one-shot tasks (e.g. '2030-01-01T00:00:00Z').

tools.name: Edge: schedule

tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the current screen. Returns the file path and base64-encoded PNG data.

filename.description: Optional filename (default: screenshot_<timestamp>.png). Saved in workspace.

region.description: Optional region for macOS: 'selection' for interactive crop, 'window' for front window. Ignored on Linux.

tools.name: Edge: screenshot

tools.description: Execute a shell command in the workspace directory

approved.description: Set true to explicitly approve medium/high-risk commands in supervised mode

tools.name: Edge: shell

tools.description: Switch to a different AI provider/model by updating config.toml. Use when current provider is rate-limited or when user explicitly requests a specific provider for a task. The change persists across requests.

reason.description: Reason for switching (for logging and user notification)

tools.name: Edge: switch_provider

tools.description:
Manage a task checklist for the current session. Use to break complex work into steps and track progress.
Actions: create (batch), add (single), update (change status), list (view all), delete (clear all).

id.description: For 'update': ID of the task to update

tasks.description: For 'create': list of tasks to create (replaces existing list)

title.description: For 'add': title of the new task

tools.name: Edge: task_plan

tools.description: Inspect and update shared network URL access policy ([security.url_access]) including first-visit approval, global allowlist/blocklist, and approved domains.
tools.name: Edge: web_access_config

tools.description: Fetch a web page and return text content for LLM consumption. Strips navigation, scripts, and boilerplate before extraction. Providers: nanohtml2text (default), firecrawl, tavily. Deprecated alias: fast_html2md. Security: allowlist-only domains, blocked_domains, and no local/private hosts.

url.description: The HTTP or HTTPS URL to fetch

tools.name: Edge: web_fetch

tools.description: Inspect and update [web_search] configuration at runtime (providers, fallbacks, retries, provider-specific keys/options).
tools.name: Edge: web_search_config

tools.description: Extract plain text and numeric data from an XLSX (Excel) file in the workspace. Returns tab-separated cell values per row for each sheet. No formulas, charts, styles, or merged-cell awareness.

path.description: Path to the XLSX file. Relative paths resolve from workspace.

tools.name: Edge: xlsx_read
